<content>1: TASK: List all available MCP tools. Report which ones start with mcp__playwright. If none, say FAIL: no playwright MCP tools found.
2: 
3: 
4: SPAWN TIER: full
5: 
6: 📚 FULL TIER: This spawn requires SYNTHESIS.md for knowledge externalization.
7:    Document your findings, decisions, and learnings in SYNTHESIS.md before completing.
8: 
9: 
10: 
11: ## CONFIG RESOLUTION
12: 
13: - Backend: opencode (source: cli-flag)
14: - Model: openai/gpt-5.2-codex (source: cli-flag)
15: - Tier: full (source: heuristic (skill-default))
16: - Spawn Mode: headless (source: default)
17: - MCP: playwright (source: cli-flag)
18: - Mode: tdd (source: default)
19: - Validation: tests (source: default)
20: 
21: 
22: 
23: 
24: ## PRIOR KNOWLEDGE (from kb context)
25: 
26: **Query:** "list all available"
27: 
28: ### Constraints (MUST respect)
29: - orch-go agent state exists in four layers (OpenCode memory, OpenCode disk, registry, tmux)
30:   - Reason: Each layer has independent lifecycle - cleanup must touch all layers or ghosts accumulate
31: - Registry is caching layer, not source of truth - all data exists in OpenCode/tmux/beads
32:   - Reason: Investigation found all registry data can be derived from primary sources
33: - OpenCode x-opencode-directory header returns ALL disk sessions, not just matching ones
34:   - Reason: API behavior is counterintuitive - without header returns in-memory only
35: - orch status counts ALL workers-* tmux windows as active
36:   - Reason: Discovered during phantom agent investigation - status inflated by persistent windows
37: - Skillc embeds ALL template_sources unconditionally
38:   - Reason: No mechanism exists for conditional inclusion at compile time. Solution must work at spawn-time (runtime reference) not compile-time (conditional includes).
39: - Worker spawns must set ORCH_WORKER=1 to skip orchestrator skill loading
40:   - Reason: Orchestrator skill (1,251 lines ~37k tokens) is auto-loaded by session-context plugin for all orch projects but is unnecessary for worker sessions, wastes context budget
41: - Dashboard must be fully usable at 666px width (half MacBook Pro screen). No horizontal scrolling. All critical info visible without scrolling.
42:   - Reason: Primary workflow is orchestrator CLI + dashboard side-by-side on MacBook Pro. Minimum width constraint - should expand gracefully on larger displays.
43: 
44: ### Prior Decisions
45: - Iteration 8 tmux fallback verification successful
46:   - Reason: After attach mode implementation (7ca8438), all three fallback mechanisms (tail, question, status) continue to work correctly with no regressions
47: - Confidence scores in investigations are noise, not signal
48:   - Reason: Nov 2025 Codex case: 5 investigations at High/Very High confidence all reached wrong conclusions. Current distribution: 90% cluster at 85-95%, providing no discriminative value. Recommendation: Replace with structured uncertainty (tested/untested enumeration).
49: - SPAWN_CONTEXT.md is 100% redundant - generated from beads + kb context + skill + template
50:   - Reason: Investigation confirmed all content exists elsewhere and can be regenerated at spawn time
51: - Progressive disclosure for skill bloat
52:   - Reason: 77% reduction (1757→400 lines) while preserving all guidance in reference docs
53: - beads output format uses ' - ' separator for title
54:   - Reason: All bd commands (list, ready) use this format: {beads-id} [{priority}] [{type}] {status} ... - {title}
55: - Multi-repo hydration requires healthy database
56:   - Reason: Orphaned dependencies in kb-cli blocked all database operations including multi-repo sync. Fix with 'bd doctor --fix' before attempting multi-repo setup.
57: - Headless spawn mode is production-ready
58:   - Reason: All 5 requirements verified working: status detection, monitoring, completion detection, error handling, user visibility. Investigation orch-go-0r2q confirmed no blockers exist.
59: - Use existing stores for UI improvements over backend changes
60:   - Reason: All necessary data (errorEvents, agentlogEvents) already exists in frontend stores; UI-only changes are faster to implement and deploy
61: - Default spawn mode is headless with --tmux opt-in
62:   - Reason: Aligns implementation with documentation (CLAUDE.md, orchestrator skill), reduces TUI overhead for automation, tmux still available via explicit flag
63: - Document all spawn modes in CLAUDE.md
64:   - Reason: Users need to see all options (headless default, --tmux opt-in, --inline blocking) to make informed spawn choices
65: 
66: ### Models (synthesized understanding)
67: - Probe: Inventory all friction gates across spawn, completion, and daemon — assess defect-catching vs noise

81:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/PHASE4_REVIEW.md
82:   - Summary:
83:     **At N=11, the model pattern shows exceptional consistency and proven utility.** All 11 models converged on the 6-section structure without enforcement. The enable/constrain query works across every domain tested. Most significantly: **the models that emerged reveal your cognitive investment priorities** - hot paths (spawn, agent, dashboard), strategic understanding (orchestrator, daemon), and owned complexity (completion, beads integration).
84:     
85:     **Key finding:** High investigation count + model existence = **friction that refused to resolve**. The absence of models for external dependencies (kb, tmux) despite high investigation counts reveals clear ownership boundaries.
86:

89: - OpenCode Fork
90:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/opencode-fork.md
91:   - Summary:
92:     Dylan owns a fork of [sst/opencode](https://github.com/sst/opencode) at `~/Documents/personal/opencode`. The fork is 16 custom commits ahead of upstream (linear forward — all upstream changes included). Custom changes focus on **memory management** (LRU/TTL instance eviction preventing 8.4GB unbounded growth), **SSE cleanup** (idempotent teardown preventing leaked connections), **OAuth stealth mode** (Claude Max access), **ORCH_WORKER header forwarding** (worker session detection), **session metadata** (extensible key-value storage), and **session TTL** (auto-expiry with periodic cleanup). The fork is a TypeScript monorepo (Bun runtime, Hono HTTP framework) with sessions stored in SQLite database (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db) using Drizzle ORM with migration-based schema management. Session status (idle/busy/retry) is tracked in-memory only, lost on server restart. A `GET /session/status` endpoint already exists for querying session state.
93:     
94:     ---

131: - Session Deletion Vectors
132:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/session-deletion-vectors.md
133:   - **STALENESS WARNING:**
134:     This model was last updated 2026-02-14.
135:     Changed files: cmd/orch/clean_cmd.go, pkg/daemon/daemon.go.
136:     Deleted files: opencode/src/session/index.ts, opencode/src/config/config.ts, opencode/src/project/instance.ts, opencode/src/session/session.sql.ts, pkg/cleanup/sessions.go, ~/bin/disk-cleanup.sh.
137:     Verify model claims about these files against current code.
138:   - Summary:
139:     Active OpenCode sessions can become unfindable through **7 independent vectors** spanning 3 systems (disk-cleanup.sh, orch-go cleanup, OpenCode itself). The fundamental problem is that no "session is active, do not touch" lock exists, and multiple processes can delete sessions from the shared SQLite database without coordination. The Ctrl+D keybind is triple-bound (app exit, session delete, input delete), creating the highest-risk accidental deletion path. The disk-cleanup.sh vector was the first confirmed root cause (now fixed), but the bug persists because at least two other vectors remain open.
140:     
141:     ---
142:   - Critical Invariants:
143:     1. **Sessions exist in SQLite or they don't** - There is no "evicted but recoverable" state
144:     2. **NotFoundError = row deleted from DB** - Not a caching issue
145:     3. **Multiple processes share one SQLite DB** - No coordination protocol
146:     4. **Cascade deletes propagate silently** - Deleting a session kills all messages and parts
147:     5. **No "active session" lock exists** - Any process can delete any session at any time
148:     6. **JSON→SQLite migration is one-time** - Gate checks `opencode.db` existence, not whether sessions were imported. DB existed from Jan 27 schema migrations → 188 JSON sessions permanently orphaned, invisible to current code
149:     
150:     ---
151:   - Why This Fails:
152:     ### Failure Mode 1: Untracked Session Deletion (Vector #2)
153:     
154:     **Symptom:** Interactive TUI session disappears mid-conversation with NotFoundError
155:     
156:     **Root cause:** `cleanUntrackedDiskSessions()` in `clean_cmd.go:408-539` finds sessions not tracked by any `.orch/workspace/*/session_id` file, checks if they were updated in the last 5 minutes, and deletes them if idle.
157:     
158:     **Why TUI sessions are vulnerable:**
159:     - Interactive/orchestrator TUI sessions have NO workspace directory
160:     - No `.session_id` file means Layer 1 protection is bypassed entirely
161:     - If the user hasn't sent a message in >5 minutes (reading, thinking, context switch), Layer 2 (recency) marks it as orphaned
162:     - Layer 3 (`IsSessionProcessing()`) only runs for recently-active sessions
163:     - Session gets deleted via `client.DeleteSession(session.ID)` at line 525
164:     
165:     **Code path:**
166:     ```
167:     orch clean --sessions (or --all)
168:       → cleanUntrackedDiskSessions()
169:         → !trackedSessionIDs[session.ID]  ← TUI has no workspace, always true
170:         → now.Sub(updatedAt) > 5min       ← User paused, true
171:         → (skips IsSessionProcessing)     ← Only checked for recent sessions
172:         → client.DeleteSession(session.ID) ← SESSION DELETED
173:     ```
174:     
175:     **Fix needed:** Call `IsSessionProcessing()` for ALL untracked sessions, not just recently active ones. Cost: one API call per untracked session.
176:     
177:     ### Failure Mode 2: Accidental Ctrl+D Deletion (Vector #3)
178:     
179:     **Symptom:** Session vanishes after user presses Ctrl+D
180:     
181:     **Root cause:** Three keybinds share `ctrl+d`:
182:     - `app_exit: "ctrl+c,ctrl+d,<leader>q"` (config.ts:771)
183:     - `session_delete: "ctrl+d"` (config.ts:784)
184:     - `input_delete: "ctrl+d,delete,shift+delete"` (config.ts:878)
185:     
186:     **Why it happens:**
187:     1. User opens session list (`<leader>l`)
188:     2. User wants to exit the list, presses Ctrl+D (habit from terminal/vim)
189:     3. Session list dialog intercepts as `session_delete`, highlights session in red
190:     4. If user presses Ctrl+D again (common stutter or habit), session is permanently deleted
191:     5. TUI crashes with NotFoundError on next render cycle
192:     
193:     The confirmation ("Press ctrl+d again to confirm") is displayed as red-highlighted title text that may not be noticed in a fast interaction.
194:     
195:     **Fix needed:** Rebind `session_delete` to a non-conflicting key, or add a modal confirmation dialog.
196:     
197:     ### Failure Mode 3: External Process Deletion (Vector #4)
198:     
199:     **Symptom:** Session disappears without user action
200:     
201:     **Root cause:** `DELETE /session/:id` route has no authentication and no coordination. Any local process can delete
202:     ... [truncated]

209: - System Learning Loop
210:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/system-learning-loop.md
211:   - Summary:
212:     The System Learning Loop is the third layer of the Pressure Visibility System that automatically converts recurring context gaps into actionable improvements. It tracks gaps during spawns, identifies patterns using RecurrenceThreshold=3, and suggests specific actions (kn entries, beads issues, investigations). The system uses shell-aware command parsing to generate runnable commands with proper quoted string handling, and ensures minimum length requirements for downstream tools (kn requires 20+ chars). This creates a closed feedback loop: gaps → patterns → suggestions → improvements → fewer gaps.
213:     
214:     ---
215:   - Critical Invariants:
216:     1. **RecurrenceThreshold = 3** - Pattern detection balances noise (1) vs signal (3+)
217:     2. **All matching events must be marked resolved** - Not just the most recent one
218:     3. **FindRecurringGaps excludes resolved events** - Prevents resolved gaps from reappearing
219:     4. **Shell-aware command parsing required** - Quoted strings with spaces must be preserved
220:     5. **Minimum 20-character reasons** - kn decide/constrain requirement enforced at generation time
221:     6. **30-day retention window** - Gap events older than 30 days are pruned
222:     7. **Gap recording happens after gating** - Captures all gaps whether spawn proceeds or not
223:     
224:     ---
225:   - Why This Fails:
226:     ### Failure Mode 1: Resolved Gaps Keep Appearing
227:     
228:     **Symptom:** `orch learn` shows same gap after running `orch learn act` to resolve it
229:     
230:     **Root cause:** Two bugs combined:
231:     1. `RecordResolution` only marked the most recent event (used `break` after first match)
232:     2. `FindRecurringGaps` counted all events without filtering by Resolution field
233:     
234:     **Why it happens:**
235:     - Gap occurs 5 times → user resolves → only 1 event marked
236:     - Next `FindRecurringGaps` call counts 4 unresolved events → still above threshold (3)
237:     - Same pattern keeps appearing despite resolution
238:     
239:     **Impact:**
240:     - User frustration ("I already fixed this!")
241:     - Loss of trust in learning system
242:     - Duplicate work
243:     
244:     **Fix:**
245:     - `RecordResolution` now marks ALL matching events (removed `break`)
246:     - `FindRecurringGaps` filters out resolved events before counting
247:     
248:     **Source:** `.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-orch-learn-resolved-gaps-still.md`
249:     
250:     ---
251:     
252:     ### Failure Mode 2: Generated Commands Fail Due to Broken Quoting
253:     
254:     **Symptom:** `orch learn act N` generates command that fails when executed
255:     
256:     **Root cause:** Using `strings.Fields()` to parse commands - splits on whitespace without respecting quotes
257:     
258:     **Why it happens:**
259:     - Command: `kn decide "auth" --reason "Used by: investigation. Occurred 5 times"`
260:     - `strings.Fields` splits into: `["kn", "decide", "\"auth\"", "--reason", "\"Used", "by:", "investigation.", ...]`
261:     - Shell receives mangled arguments, command fails
262:     
263:     **Impact:**
264:     - Learning loop broken - suggestions can't be executed
265:     - User must manually reconstruct commands
266:     - Reduces value of automated suggestions
267:     
268:     **Fix:**
269:     - Added `ParseShellCommand()` with shell-aware quote handling
270:     - Respects double and single quotes as argument delimiters
271:     - Added `ValidateCommand()` to catch malformed commands before execution
272:     
273:     **Source:** `.kb/investigations/2025-12-26-inv-orch-learn-act-commands-should.md`
274:     
275:     ---
276:     
277:     ### Failure Mode 3: Short Reasons Fail kn Validation
278:     
279:     **Symptom:** `orch learn act` generates kn command that fails with "reason too short" error
280:     
281:     **Root cause:** `generateReasonFromGaps` produced "Occurred N times" (16 chars) when gap events lacked skill/task metadata
282:     
283:     **Why it happens:**
284:     - Gap events with sparse metadata → only occurrence count available
285:     - "Occurred 3 times" = 16 characters
286:     - kn requires 20+ characters for `--reason` flag
287:     - Command fails validation at execution time
288:     
289:     **Impact:**
290:     - Generated commands unusable
291:     - User must manually edit reason strings
292:     - Learning loop broken for sparse gaps
293:     
294:     **Fix:**
295:     -
296:     ... [truncated]

part.text:
**Checking usage for adding comments**

I'm reviewing the help output for the comment-adding command to understand how to specify the required issue ID since it wasn't provided.

state.title: Show bd comments add usage
state.output:
Add a comment to an issue.

Examples:
  # Add a comment
  bd comments add bd-123 "Working on this now"

  # Add a comment from a file
  bd comments add bd-123 -f notes.txt

Usage:
  bd comments add [issue-id] [text] [flags]

Flags:
  -a, --author string   Add author to comment
  -f, --file string     Read comment text from file
  -h, --help            help for add

Global Flags:
      --actor string            Actor name for audit trail (default: $BD_ACTOR or $USER)
      --allow-stale             Allow operations on potentially stale data (skip staleness check)
      --db string               Database path (default: auto-discover .beads/*.db)
      --json                    Output in JSON format
      --lock-timeout duration   SQLite busy timeout (0 = fail immediately if locked) (default 30s)
      --no-auto-flush           Disable automatic JSONL sync after CRUD operations
      --no-auto-import          Disable automatic JSONL import when newer than DB
      --no-daemon               Force direct storage mode, bypass daemon if running
      --no-db                   JSONL-only mode (default, kept for backwards compatibility)
      --profile                 Generate CPU profile for performance analysis
  -q, --quiet                   Suppress non-essential output (errors only)
      --readonly                Read-only mode: block write operations (for worker sandboxes)
      --sandbox                 Sandbox mode: disables daemon and auto-sync
      --sqlite                  Use SQLite database (opt-in, JSONL-only is default)
  -v, --verbose                 Enable verbose/debug output
